name: obsidian-bases
description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
capabilities: Create valid Obsidian .base files with proper YAML structure
Define table, cards, list, and map views for note collections
Write filters using logical operators (and/or/not) and property conditions
Create formulas with date arithmetic, string manipulation, and conditional logic
Configure custom summaries like Average, Min, Max, Sum, and Median
Embed bases in notes and reference specific views
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Static analyzer detected 259 patterns (233 external_commands, 4 network, 1 filesystem) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only describing YAML syntax for Obsidian Bases. Backticks are Markdown code formatting, not shell execution. URLs are reference links to Obsidian documentation. No executable code, network calls, or filesystem operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Organize your Obsidian notes with dynamic database-like views. This skill enables you to create .base files with table, card, list, and map views, complete with filters, formulas, and custom summaries.
limitations: Only works with Obsidian Bases feature - requires Obsidian app installation
Cannot execute or render bases - only creates and edits the YAML files
Does not validate formulas against actual vault data
Map views require the Maps community plugin to function

useCases.title: Task Management Dashboard
useCases.description: Create a base file that displays tasks from multiple notes in a unified table view with status grouping, priority labels, and due date calculations.

useCases.title: Reading List Library
useCases.description: Build a card-view base showing books and articles with cover images, author information, reading status, and estimated reading time formulas.

useCases.title: Project Notes Index
useCases.description: Design a base that aggregates project notes with last-updated timestamps, link counts, and folder-based filtering for team knowledge bases.

promptTemplates.title: Create a Simple Table View
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a .base file that shows all notes tagged with 'meeting' in a table view with columns for file name, date, and attendees.

promptTemplates.title: Build a Task Tracker Base
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a task tracker base with two views: one table showing active tasks grouped by status, and another showing completed tasks. Include formulas for days until due and priority labels.

promptTemplates.title: Design a Reading Library Card View
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a base file for my reading list with a cards view showing book covers, author, status icon, and estimated reading time. Filter out books marked as 'dropped'.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Project Dashboard with Summaries
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive project dashboard base with global filters for the Projects folder, formulas for last updated relative time and link counts, groupBy status, and custom summary calculations for average links per project.

bestPractices: Use single quotes for formula expressions containing double quotes to avoid YAML parsing issues
Start with global filters for conditions that apply to all views, then add view-specific filters for refinements
Test formulas incrementally - verify simple expressions before combining into complex calculations
antiPatterns: Do not create overly complex nested filters - break them into readable and/or/not blocks
Avoid using file paths directly in formulas - use file properties like file.folder instead
Do not forget to quote strings containing special characters or comparison operators

faq.question: What is an Obsidian Base file?
faq.answer: A Base file (.base extension) is a YAML file that defines dynamic views of notes in your Obsidian vault. It can contain filters, formulas, and multiple view types like tables, cards, lists, or maps.

faq.question: How do I view a Base file in Obsidian?
faq.answer: Embed the base file in any note using ![[filename.base]] syntax, or reference a specific view with ![[filename.base#View Name]].

faq.question: What is the difference between global filters and view filters?
faq.answer: Global filters apply to all views in the base file. View filters are defined within a specific view and only affect that view's results.

faq.question: Can I use formulas in filters?
faq.answer: Formulas are defined separately in the formulas section and referenced by name. Filters use expressions that can reference formula properties using formula.formula_name syntax.

faq.question: How do I group results in a table view?
faq.answer: Add a groupBy property to your view with the property name and direction (ASC or DESC). Results will be grouped by that property's values.

faq.question: What summary formulas are available?
faq.answer: Built-in summaries include Average, Min, Max, Sum, Range, Median, Stddev for numbers; Earliest, Latest for dates; and Checked, Unchecked, Empty, Filled, Unique for any type.
